legal_compliance_checklist.description: Declare personal-data processing to INPDP and get prior authorization when the treatment falls into authorization cases.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: privacy notice and user rights process
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Tell users what data you collect, why you collect it, and how they can access, correct, or delete it.
Check foreign hosting or foreign SaaS tools because personal-data transfer abroad needs INPDP authorization.
Maintain basic security controls, logging, backup, and an internal response process for cyber incidents.
If the product uses online contracts, electronic signatures, or certified digital documents, align with the Tunisian e-commerce and certification framework.

trust_requirements.description: Show users what data is collected and how it is used.
Protect accounts, user data, and core systems with basic security measures.
Keep the service stable and give users a clear support contact.
Make pricing, renewal, cancellation, and platform rules easy to understand.

special_operational_constraints.description: Using hosting or tools outside Tunisia can create data-transfer approval work.

special_operational_constraints.item: mandatory cyber audit for some operators
special_operational_constraints.description: Hosting providers, cloud providers, telecom or internet operators, and some services processing user data over telecom networks can fall into mandatory cyber audit and classification rules.

special_operational_constraints.item: certification and signature services are regulated
special_operational_constraints.description: If the product offers certified signature, trust services, or similar functions, extra approvals and technical rules apply.

special_operational_constraints.item: security obligations rise fast with sensitivity
special_operational_constraints.description: Risk increases if the product handles sensitive personal data, critical services, or high-impact infrastructure.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Confirm the right authority before launch because private school, private higher education, and private vocational training do not use the same approval path.
Do not market the offer as officially recognized, accredited, or diploma-granting unless the status is actually approved.
Set up privacy notice, consent handling, access or correction process, and INPDP data-processing compliance for student and parent data.
Make fees, schedule, cancellation, delivery format, and certificate conditions clear in writing.
Maintain basic learner protection, incident handling, and site or session safety procedures.

trust_requirements.description: Learners must understand whether the program is informal, private training, or officially authorized education.
Use credible instructors and make their role and expertise clear.
Protect learners from abuse, disorder, and unsafe learning conditions.
Keep attendance, progress, certificates, and schedule commitments consistent.
Use clear communication and consent handling when minors are involved.

special_operational_constraints.description: A tutoring app, private school, private university, and private training center do not face the same regulator or approval process.
Working with children increases safeguarding, consent, and parent communication needs.
School-like or degree-like models face more operational and approval constraints than simple adult short-course offers.

special_operational_constraints.item: student data can create transfer issues
special_operational_constraints.description: If student or parent data is hosted or processed abroad, cross-border data-transfer approval can become necessary.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Confirm that the activity, facility, and health professionals are legally authorized before launch.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: patient data and medical secrecy compliance
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Set up INPDP data-processing compliance, confidentiality controls, and restricted access to patient data.
Use clear consent, maintain patient records, and document care or service actions.
Handle care-related waste, hygiene, and hazardous materials with the required process.
Do not make misleading medical claims and keep public communication consistent with health-profession rules.

trust_requirements.description: Patients must trust that care is provided by qualified and authorized people.
Protect patient information and keep medical discussions private.
Show reliable hygiene, safety, and handling standards.
Explain what the service can do, what it cannot do, and when referral or urgent escalation is needed.
Keep appointments, results, records, and patient communication consistent.

special_operational_constraints.description: Clinics, labs, imaging centers, hemodialysis centers, medical transport, and medicine manufacturing do not follow the same approval path.
Health data has stricter handling rules and can only be processed by doctors or people bound by professional secrecy.

special_operational_constraints.item: waste handling is an operational burden
special_operational_constraints.description: Sharps, biological waste, chemical waste, pharmaceutical waste, and other hazardous health waste need controlled handling.

special_operational_constraints.item: digital health still carries health-sector duties
special_operational_constraints.description: Web, media, and digital channels in health still require deontological care, privacy protection, and controlled communication.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Confirm the activity is legally allowed before launch because banking, payment services, insurance, lending, exchange, and investment activity do not use the same regulator.
Set up customer due diligence, beneficial owner checks, risk scoring, recordkeeping, and suspicious transaction reporting.
Make pricing, fees, repayment or settlement terms, and complaint path clear in writing.
Protect customer financial data, define access controls, and check INPDP obligations for data processing and foreign data transfer.
Keep records for transactions, account actions, approvals, and exception handling.

trust_requirements.description: Customers must trust that the business is allowed to handle the financial activity it offers.
Protect accounts, payments, credentials, and sensitive financial data.
Keep financial records, statements, and transaction history consistent.
Show charges clearly and provide a reliable path for disputes or errors.
Keep payment, account, and support functions available and controlled.

special_operational_constraints.description: Banking, payment services, insurance, securities, lending, and exchange are not governed in the same way.
If the business holds funds, moves money, pays out users, or handles exchange activity, the compliance burden rises fast.

special_operational_constraints.item: investment solicitation can trigger CMF rules
special_operational_constraints.description: Public investment offers, securities activity, or fundraising from the public can create capital-markets obligations.
Insurance and related distribution activity face their own supervision and sector controls.

special_operational_constraints.item: cross-border and foreign-currency activity adds complexity
special_operational_constraints.description: Foreign transfers, foreign hosting, and exchange-related operations can create extra approvals and monitoring needs.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Clearly show product characteristics, price, and sale conditions to customers.
Provide invoices and handle guarantee or warranty obligations correctly.
If sales happen online, comply with Tunisia's electronic commerce framework and online consumer information duties.
Declare periodic or seasonal discount operations when the sale format requires it.
Respect competition, pricing, and commercial practice rules in normal sales activity.

trust_requirements.description: Customers must easily understand the real price and sales conditions.
Give accurate information about what is being sold and under what terms.
Handle delivery problems, returns, warranty issues, and complaints in a clear way.
Customers should receive invoices or purchase proof and know how to claim support.

special_operational_constraints.description: Seasonal or periodic sales are not fully informal and may require declaration before launch.
Selling through websites or electronic channels adds electronic transaction and online information obligations.

special_operational_constraints.item: some trade activities need extra approvals
special_operational_constraints.description: Certain products, activities, or sale formats can require cahiers des charges, authorizations, or specific sector rules.
Commercial activity is subject to regular economic control and consumer protection oversight.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the activity needs a transport authorization, permit, or cahier des charges before launch.
Use compliant vehicles and keep required registration, technical, and professional qualification documents in order.
If goods cross borders, verify customs classification, required documents, and applicable import or export rules.
If the business tracks drivers, riders, or shipments, comply with Tunisia's personal data rules.
If booking, dispatch, payment, or delivery proof happens online, comply with Tunisia's electronic commerce framework.

trust_requirements.description: Users must understand fares, fees, delivery terms, and who is responsible if something goes wrong.
Keep clear records of trips, orders, dispatches, and delivery or service completion.
Handle delays, lost goods, damage, and safety incidents with a clear process.
Customers and partners expect controlled operations, trained staff, and dependable handoff procedures.

special_operational_constraints.description: Passenger transport, goods transport, rental, platform-enabled mobility, and international transport do not all follow the same rules.

special_operational_constraints.item: some transport activities use permits and controlled categories
special_operational_constraints.description: Road transport activities can depend on approvals, route or category limits, and sector-specific operating conditions.
International movement of goods or vehicles adds customs checks, trade documents, and border procedures.
Hazardous materials transport has a separate safety regime and should be treated as a higher-complexity case.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Verify the legal status of the property, title registration, and any required land-record updates before sale, development, or formal transfer.
Use contracts and supporting documents that match the activity model, especially for rental, sale, and managed occupancy relationships.
Confirm zoning, local urbanism rules, and whether the project needs municipal authorization such as a building permit before works or launch.
Check building safety and fire-prevention obligations when operating, fitting out, or opening premises used by tenants, buyers, staff, or the public.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: personal data and business record compliance
legal_compliance_checklist.description: If you collect tenant, buyer, owner, or platform-user data, follow Tunisia's personal-data rules and keep business records and accounting documents properly.

trust_requirements.description: Customers and partners need confidence that the property, title, and occupancy situation are real and correctly documented.
Rent, deposits, fees, duration, handover conditions, and responsibilities should be easy to understand before commitment.
Keep a reliable trail for contracts, payments, notices, approvals, and title-related documents.
Tenant and buyer data should be collected, stored, and shared carefully.

special_operational_constraints.item: property status is a core legal dependency
special_operational_constraints.description: A real estate or housing business can fail on a single bad title, registration issue, or unresolved land-status problem.

special_operational_constraints.item: local rules can change the project
special_operational_constraints.description: Municipal and urbanism constraints can differ by zone, land type, and project type, so the same model may not be launchable everywhere.

special_operational_constraints.item: rental sub-types are not all the same
special_operational_constraints.description: Residential, commercial, and other occupancy arrangements should not be treated as one generic lease model.

special_operational_constraints.item: physical premises create extra compliance load
special_operational_constraints.description: Once the business controls or operates buildings, premises safety, fit-out, and building-use conformity become operational issues, not just paperwork.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Follow hygiene and sanitation rules for food premises, staff practices, storage, cleaning, and waste handling.
Make sure food sold or served is safe, properly handled, and kept under suitable storage and transport conditions.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: consumer information and proof of sale
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Clearly show price, product information, and sale conditions, and provide invoices or sale proof when required.
If the business produces, transforms, stores, or packs animal-origin food, check veterinary sanitary approval or registration requirements.
Check whether the activity, premises, or sale format needs a local authorization, declaration, or cahier des charges before opening.

trust_requirements.description: Customers must trust that food is prepared, stored, and served in safe conditions.
Customers should easily understand what they are buying, its key characteristics, and the real price.
Perishable products need visible discipline on freshness, storage, and delivery conditions.
Problems involving quality, safety, spoiled goods, or service failure should be handled fast and clearly.

special_operational_constraints.item: sub-sectors are not all regulated the same way
special_operational_constraints.description: Restaurants, cafes, packaged food, catering, food delivery, and food processing should not be treated as one identical case.
Meat, dairy, fish, and similar products face stricter sanitary and veterinary control than lower-risk food categories.

special_operational_constraints.item: premises hygiene is a real enforcement issue
special_operational_constraints.description: Food businesses can face direct control around hygiene, cleanliness, and public-health conditions at the operating site.

special_operational_constraints.item: cold chain failures create major risk
special_operational_constraints.description: Businesses handling perishable products need tighter discipline on storage, transport, and timing.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: activity-specific authorization or cahier des charges check
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the sub-activity must follow a cahier des charges, approval, or sector-specific opening condition before launch.
Keep the site compliant for hygiene, cleanliness, client safety, and suitable operating conditions.
If the business runs a gym or sports space, verify required insurance, accident prevention, and operating conditions.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: product compliance for cosmetics or supplements
legal_compliance_checklist.description: If you import, sell, or use cosmetic products or supplements, check product conformity and applicable import or control procedures.
Do not treat invasive aesthetic care, medical procedures, or clinical treatment as ordinary beauty services without checking health-sector rules.

trust_requirements.description: Clients need confidence that tools, spaces, and handling practices are clean and controlled.
Clients should understand what the service does, what it does not do, and any relevant limits or risks.
Service quality depends on staff using safe methods and following consistent procedures.
Clients should understand pricing, session terms, refund conditions, and how incidents are handled.

special_operational_constraints.description: Hairdressing, spas, thermal establishments, gyms, wellness studios, and aesthetic services should not be treated as one identical case.

special_operational_constraints.item: medical aesthetics can move into health regulation
special_operational_constraints.description: Injectables, clinical treatments, invasive procedures, and similar services can fall under stricter medical or private-health rules.
Cosmetics, supplements, and related products can trigger conformity, import-control, or health-related checks.
Gyms and supervised physical activities create a higher need for insurance, safety discipline, and incident prevention.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Make sure published or distributed content follows Tunisia's press and publication rules.
If the activity includes radio, TV, or similar broadcast media, check HAICA licensing and applicable cahiers des charges before launch.
Ads, promotions, and public communications should be clear, non-misleading, and compliant with consumer protection rules.
Secure the rights to use music, video, images, articles, brand assets, and other protected content.
If you collect audience, subscriber, campaign, or contact data, follow Tunisia's personal-data rules.

trust_requirements.description: Audiences and clients need confidence that published information is controlled and not misleading.
People should be able to distinguish editorial, promotional, and paid communication.
Clients and partners need confidence that content is used with proper permission.
The business should be able to respond clearly to complaints, takedown requests, and correction issues.

special_operational_constraints.description: Print, digital media, audiovisual media, advertising, PR, and creator-style content should not be treated as one identical case.
Broadcast-style media can require licensing, regulator interaction, and stricter operating conditions.
Defamation, unlawful content, political sensitivity, and rights misuse can turn a normal media activity into a higher-risk case.

special_operational_constraints.item: audience data and tracking add compliance load
special_operational_constraints.description: Mailing lists, campaign databases, analytics, and audience profiling create extra data-handling obligations.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the activity is a regulated profession that requires registration, authorization, or membership in a professional order before launch.
Set up the activity with the correct legal, tax, invoicing, and social-security treatment for an independent or service business.
Use clear engagement terms covering scope, deliverables, fees, responsibility limits, and payment conditions.
Protect client information and follow Tunisia's personal-data rules when handling client files, contacts, or operational records.
If the activity falls into a designated profession or handles sensitive financial transactions, verify anti-money-laundering obligations.

trust_requirements.description: Clients need confidence that the provider is actually qualified to give the promised service.
Clients should understand exactly what is included, excluded, and expected from the service.
Professional services depend on disciplined handling of sensitive documents, advice, and communications.
Keep a clear record of missions, outputs, approvals, invoices, and client communications.

special_operational_constraints.description: Consulting, legal, accounting, architecture, engineering, and other advisory services should not be treated as one identical case.
Certain services can only be provided by licensed professionals or members of an official professional order.
A service business can face serious exposure if clients rely on incorrect advice, filings, or technical outputs.

special_operational_constraints.item: financial or legal handling can raise compliance level
special_operational_constraints.description: Once the service touches regulated filings, financial flows, or high-sensitivity client matters, compliance expectations increase.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the unit needs an environmental impact study, an environmental cahier des charges, or other pre-launch administrative approvals.
Control industrial discharges, wastewater pre-treatment, and pollution sources according to applicable limits.
Sort, store, move, and dispose of industrial or hazardous waste through compliant channels and required documents.
Apply workplace health, safety, accident-prevention, and occupational-risk controls for staff, machines, and facilities.
Check whether products, inputs, or imported equipment are subject to standards, certification, or technical control before sale or use.

trust_requirements.description: Customers and partners need confidence that output is stable and matches the promised specification.
Keep clear records for raw materials, production lots, controls, and non-conformities.
Workers, visitors, and nearby parties expect disciplined handling of machines, chemicals, heat, and other industrial risks.
Maintain inspection, maintenance, safety, and waste records in a usable form.

special_operational_constraints.description: Light assembly, chemicals, food processing, packaging, construction materials, and heavy industry should not be treated as one identical case.
Projects with stronger environmental impact can trigger EIA, discharge controls, and stricter operating conditions.
Chemicals, flammables, high heat, pressure systems, or explosion risk raise the required safety level.

special_operational_constraints.item: some products need extra conformity checks
special_operational_constraints.description: Certain manufactured goods face specific conformity, certification, or import/export control before market placement.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: agricultural land and land-use status check
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Verify the legal status of the land and do not assume agricultural land can be repurposed or built on freely.
Check irrigation rights, water-use limits, and any local constraints before relying on irrigated production.
Use compliant plant-protection practices and approved pesticides or treatment methods where required.
Use seeds, plants, or varieties that meet Tunisia's certification, control, or import rules when applicable.
If the activity includes livestock or animal products, check animal-health, sanitary, and establishment-approval requirements.

trust_requirements.description: Buyers and partners need confidence in how products were grown, treated, stored, and moved.
Customers and downstream partners expect disciplined use of pesticides, treatments, feed, and similar inputs.
The business should be able to show product origin, lot identity, and basic quality condition.
Operations should detect, report, and handle disease or infestation issues in a controlled way.

special_operational_constraints.description: Field crops, irrigated farming, livestock, nurseries, export crops, and animal-product activities should not be treated as one identical case.

special_operational_constraints.item: water dependency can become a legal and operating constraint
special_operational_constraints.description: A model that depends on irrigation may face real limits from water allocation, local infrastructure, or water-stress conditions.

special_operational_constraints.item: protected agricultural land is not fully flexible
special_operational_constraints.description: Land conversion, non-agricultural construction, and some side activities can require special authorization.

special_operational_constraints.item: export or animal-linked activities raise the compliance level
special_operational_constraints.description: Once the business exports plants or handles animals or animal products, phytosanitary or veterinary control becomes more demanding.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the project needs a building permit or other municipal approval and confirm zoning and urbanism compliance before works.
Verify whether the activity, specialty, or contractor status requires a cahier des charges, approval, or recognized qualification.
Apply workplace safety rules for worksites, tools, machinery, heights, and accident prevention.
Respect fire-prevention and building-safety rules when works affect occupied premises or building systems.
Handle construction waste, debris, and hazardous materials through compliant collection, storage, and disposal practices.

trust_requirements.description: Clients should understand exactly what work is included, what materials are used, and what the final price covers.
Customers need confidence that works are done safely, correctly, and by people who know the job.
Keep a clear record of approvals, interventions, materials, changes, and handover.
Problems, delays, defects, and return visits should be handled with a clear process.

special_operational_constraints.description: General construction, renovation, plumbing, electrical work, HVAC, maintenance, and property-related home services should not be treated as one identical case.

special_operational_constraints.item: structural or technical works raise the compliance level
special_operational_constraints.description: Structural works and safety-sensitive trades such as electrical, gas, or major equipment installation need tighter control than light home services.

special_operational_constraints.item: municipal rules can block or reshape the project
special_operational_constraints.description: The same project may be allowed in one location and blocked or conditioned in another because of local permit and urbanism rules.

special_operational_constraints.item: public or larger works are more controlled
special_operational_constraints.description: Public works, larger sites, and contractor participation in formal tenders face stricter qualification and operating requirements than small household jobs.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: sector authorization and operating regime check
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the activity needs an authorization, concession, or other sector-specific regime before launch.
Verify whether the project needs an environmental impact study, environmental cahier des charges, or other pre-launch environmental approval.
Use compliant equipment, installations, and safety measures for energy, industrial, or environmental operations.
If the activity collects, transports, treats, recycles, or stores waste or hazardous materials, check the required authorization and movement documents.
Control emissions, discharges, and pollution sources and verify any monitoring or reporting duties that apply to the site or process.

trust_requirements.description: Customers, partners, and authorities need confidence that systems operate safely and as promised.
Environmental benefit, efficiency, recycling, or decarbonization claims should be real and supportable.
Keep clear records for installations, maintenance, waste flows, incidents, and compliance actions.
The business should handle outages, pollution events, safety incidents, and service failures with a clear process.

special_operational_constraints.description: Renewable energy, energy services, waste management, recycling, pollution control, and environmental monitoring should not be treated as one identical case.
Projects tied to electricity production, grid injection, or large installations face tighter approval and technical constraints.

special_operational_constraints.item: waste activities can require formal authorization
special_operational_constraints.description: Collection, transport, treatment, and recycling are not fully informal activities and can depend on authorization or cahier des charges.

special_operational_constraints.item: high-risk materials raise the compliance level
special_operational_constraints.description: Dangerous waste, batteries, chemicals, gas, fuel, or other hazardous inputs increase the required safety and control level.

legal_compliance_checklist.item: tourism activity authorization or classification check
legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the business needs a tourism-sector authorization, cahier des charges, or official classification before launch.
Keep hotels, guest spaces, restaurants, and public-facing premises compliant for fire prevention, safety, and public use.
Clearly show prices, booking terms, cancellation conditions, and what the customer is actually buying.
If the business serves food or drinks, follow hygiene, sanitation, storage, and food-safety rules.
If you collect guest, traveler, or booking data, follow Tunisia's personal-data rules.

trust_requirements.description: Customers should understand price, cancellation, refund rules, and what is included before paying.
The business should describe rooms, trips, amenities, timing, and service level accurately.
Guests need confidence that premises, activities, and service handling are safe and controlled.
Delays, overbooking, service failure, and customer complaints should be handled with a clear process.

special_operational_constraints.description: Travel agencies, hotels, guesthouses, camps, tourist restaurants, and experience operators should not be treated as one identical case.
Some hospitality activities depend on formal classification, inspection, or sector-specific operating conditions.

special_operational_constraints.item: alternative accommodation can have a separate regime
special_operational_constraints.description: Guesthouses, rural stays, motels, and camps can follow different rules from classic hotels.

special_operational_constraints.item: extra services can raise the compliance level
special_operational_constraints.description: Once the business adds transport, food service, or organized excursions, the compliance load becomes heavier.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check whether the activity needs a telecom authorization, license, concession, or other sector-specific approval before launch.
If the business uses or sells telecom or radio equipment, verify homologation, conformity, and any import-control requirements.
If the activity handles subscriber, traffic, location, or user data, follow Tunisia's personal-data rules and sector-specific handling duties.
Apply the required cybersecurity and system-protection measures for networks, platforms, and communications infrastructure.
Clearly present service terms, pricing, coverage or availability limits, and complaint or support conditions.

trust_requirements.description: Users and business clients need confidence that connectivity and communications services work consistently.
Customers should understand charges, bundles, limits, and service conditions before subscribing.
Clients expect disciplined handling of personal data, account access, and network-security risks.
Outages, degraded service, billing problems, and abuse issues should be handled with a clear process.

special_operational_constraints.description: Public networks, internet access, value-added services, infrastructure providers, and connectivity-enabled platforms should not be treated as one identical case.

special_operational_constraints.item: spectrum and radio use are controlled
special_operational_constraints.description: Wireless activity can depend on spectrum rules, equipment approval, and tighter technical oversight.
Businesses that install or operate telecom infrastructure face heavier approval, interconnection, and technical obligations than simple software businesses.
Telecom and connectivity services face stronger cybersecurity and resilience expectations because they affect communications systems and critical operations.

legal_compliance_checklist.description: Check the correct legal form and operating regime before launch, especially if the activity is an association, grant-funded entity, or public-mission operator.
Keep the required governance records, financial controls, declarations, and reporting for the chosen structure and funding model.
If the activity receives public funds or sells to public bodies, follow Tunisia's public-funding and public-procurement rules.
If you collect beneficiary, donor, citizen, staff, or program data, follow Tunisia's personal-data rules.
If the activity is public-sector or closely tied to public service, check applicable transparency, publication, and access-to-information obligations.

trust_requirements.description: Donors, authorities, and partners need confidence that money is used for the stated purpose and properly tracked.
The organization should have visible decision-making, responsibility lines, and documented controls.
Keep clear records of beneficiaries, activities, approvals, contracts, and reported results.
Public-interest and nonprofit activities often involve sensitive personal data and need disciplined handling.

special_operational_constraints.description: Associations, publicly funded projects, NGOs, public-service operators, and government suppliers should not be treated as one identical case.
Once the activity uses public funding or contracts with public bodies, reporting, control, and procurement expectations become heavier.

special_operational_constraints.item: foreign or grant funding can add declarations and scrutiny
special_operational_constraints.description: Nonprofit funding sources can trigger extra transparency, declaration, and governance requirements.
Approvals, public procedures, and formal reporting cycles can make this sector slower to launch and operate than normal private business.
